description:
# Why

Today balls supports a 'shared task hub' model via `state_remote` in `.balls/config.json`, written by `bl remaster <hub> --commit`. That gets you halfway: User A can commit a config that says 'this repo's task tracker lives on hub X', and a clone of the repo inherits that config.

The break is at the URL boundary. `state_remote` stores a git remote *name* ('hub'), not a URL. Remote names live in `.git/config`, which is per-clone and not tracked. So a teammate doing a fresh `git clone` lands with:

- committed config saying `state_remote=hub`
- no `hub` remote in their `.git/config`
- silent failure or manual `git remote add hub <url>` to recover

This breaks the property the model promises — 'one person configures it, everyone else inherits.' It also defeats the cleanest cross-repo composition: 'project B's tasks are mastered by project A', where A is a real other project (not a neutral hub). To make that inherit, balls has to own the URL too, not just the remote name.

The desired property: a freshly-cloned repo whose committed config names an external task-master should just work, with no manual git surgery. Specifically, **`bl prime` on a so-configured repo should set up whatever it needs to** — resolve the hub, materialize the local checkout, wire the seam — so the next `bl ready`/`claim`/`review`/`close` already sees the hub's tasks.

# Concrete gap

1. Committed config can name a remote but not its URL → fresh clones can't resolve.
2. `bl prime` doesn't auto-provision the remote/checkout from committed config; it assumes the local git state is already set up.
3. Plugin sync, lifecycle pushes, etc. all funnel through `state_remote` → silently misbehave or error when the remote name doesn't resolve.

# Desired model (sketch — open to alternatives)

A repo configured to master on another stores, in committed `.balls/config.json`:
- the hub's git URL (not just a remote name), and
- enough metadata for balls to manage the local handle itself.

`bl prime` on such a repo:
- materializes a balls-owned checkout under `.balls/` (separate from the project's `.git/` so we don't pollute user git remotes),
- fetches the hub's `balls/tasks` into it,
- becomes the source of truth for `bl list`/`ready`/`claim`/`review`/`close` instead of the project repo's own `balls/tasks`.

A silent adapter translates: when balls would normally read/write the project repo's `balls/tasks`, it instead reads/writes the hub-mastered branch in the `.balls/`-owned checkout. The project's own git is untouched.

This isolates the cross-repo mastering from the project's git config entirely — the project doesn't grow a phantom remote, and the user's `git remote -v` stays clean. The hub URL lives in committed balls config; the checkout is balls' implementation detail.

# Open architectural questions

- Is the separate-checkout-under-`.balls/` the right physical layout, or should balls reuse the project's `.git/objects` via worktree/alt-objects to avoid duplicating storage? (Worktree-style is cheaper; separate-checkout is more isolated.)
- How does this compose with the existing `state_remote` field? Probably: deprecate-in-place — `state_remote=<remote name>` still works for the 'shared hub via origin' case; a new field (e.g. `master_url`) covers the cross-clone case and supersedes it.
- Auth surface: hub URL might be ssh or token-gated https. `bl prime` needs to surface auth failures clearly (probably reuses existing plugin auth-check patterns).
- Stealth/no-git mode: probably out of scope — cross-repo mastering inherently requires git on at least one side.
- Plugins on the mastered side: a clone of B with `master_url=A` inherits A's plugin configs from A's `balls/tasks`, not B's committed config. Need to nail down precedence.

# Scope / non-scope

In scope:
- New committed field for hub URL.
- `bl prime` auto-provision from that field.
- Balls-owned checkout + silent adapter for the lifecycle commands.
- Migration story from current `state_remote=<name>` users (probably auto-promote on `bl remaster --commit` when target is a URL).

Out of scope (file as children if they grow legs):
- Cross-repo plugin config layering rules.
- Multi-master (federating tasks across more than two repos).
- A 'pull-only' read-only mastering mode (clone of B can see A's tasks but can't claim them).

# Origin

Came out of a conversation about whether teammates cloning a multi-repo project need to re-do plugin/master config themselves. Plugin configs DO travel (committed under `.balls/plugins/`); the cross-repo mastering pointer does NOT, because URL lives only in `.git/config`. This ball is about closing that asymmetry so the multi-repo story actually composes.
title: Persistent cross-repo task-master: committed hub URL + auto-resolving checkout
